title: 15 test files use tempfile.mkdtemp() instead of pytest tmp_path fixture
description: Multiple test files create temporary directories via tempfile.mkdtemp() without cleanup, leaking directories on every test run. The conftest.py already provides a `project` fixture using tmp_path correctly, but 15 files bypass it. Files affected: test_caption_accessibility.py (5 calls), test_themes.py (5), test_invariants_property.py (4), test_themes_extended.py (3), test_timeline_operations.py (2), test_captions_export.py (2), test_slide_import.py (1), test_device_frame.py (1), test_invariants.py (1), test_captions_import_export.py (1), test_export_campackage.py (1), test_slide_import_extended.py (1). Each mkdtemp() call creates a directory that is never removed.
recommendation: Replace `Path(tempfile.mkdtemp()) / 'test.cmproj'` with `tmp_path / 'test.cmproj'` using pytest's tmp_path fixture. This ensures automatic cleanup and consistent test isolation.

title: test_import_trec_mocked uses NamedTemporaryFile(delete=False) with manual os.unlink in finally block
description: Two tests in test_import_trec_mocked.py create temp files with delete=False and clean up via os.unlink() in a finally block. If the test crashes before reaching the finally (e.g., segfault in pymediainfo mock), the temp file leaks. Additionally, the tests use importlib.reload() to swap pymediainfo mocks, which is fragile and can leave module state corrupted for subsequent tests.
recommendation: Use tmp_path fixture: `trec = tmp_path / 'test.trec'; trec.write_bytes(b'\x00')`. This eliminates both the leak risk and the manual cleanup.

title: test_trec_import.py depends on /tmp/Anomaly Detection Demo (v3).cmproj existing
description: Three tests in test_trec_import.py (TestProbeTrec class) search for .trec files in a hardcoded path '/tmp/Anomaly Detection Demo (v3).cmproj/recordings'. If the directory doesn't exist, all three tests silently skip via pytest.skip(). This means the tests provide zero coverage in CI or on any machine without this specific project pre-staged. These are effectively dead tests that appear to pass but never actually run.
recommendation: Either: (1) create a minimal .trec fixture file in tests/fixtures/ and use it directly, or (2) mark these tests with @pytest.mark.skipif and a clear reason, and add a mocked equivalent that always runs. The current silent skip hides the fact that these tests never execute.

title: test_techsmith_roundtrip.py scans /tmp/techsmith_extra and /tmp/techsmith_samples for test data
description: _collect_samples() in test_techsmith_roundtrip.py scans two hardcoded /tmp directories for .assetproj files. If neither directory exists, the test only runs against the bundled fixtures. The parametrize list is computed at module import time, so the test count varies between environments. This makes test results non-reproducible across machines.
recommendation: Move any required sample files into tests/fixtures/ or a git-lfs-tracked directory. If the /tmp samples are optional extras, document this clearly and add a conftest marker so CI reports which samples were tested.

title: test_camtasia_validation.py patches 5 modules per test, testing mock wiring not behavior
description: Every test in TestCamtasiaValidateSuccess, TestCamtasiaValidateWithExceptions, etc. patches subprocess, time, tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile, Path.read_text, and builtins.open simultaneously. This creates a test that verifies the mock wiring rather than the actual behavior of camtasia_validate(). If the implementation changes how it reads logs (e.g., switches from Path.read_text to open()), all tests break even though behavior is unchanged.
recommendation: Consider testing camtasia_validate() at a higher level: create a real temp project directory, write a fake log file, and only mock subprocess.Popen (the external process). This tests actual file I/O behavior while still avoiding launching Camtasia.

title: test_batch_operations.py tests only mock interactions, not actual clip mutations
description: All tests in test_batch_operations.py use MagicMock clips and tracks. Tests like TestMoveAll verify that mock_clip.move_to was called with expected args, but never verify that a real clip's position actually changes. If the real Clip.move_to() method has a bug, these tests would still pass. The batch operations are thin wrappers, so the mocks test the wrapper logic but not the end-to-end behavior.
recommendation: Add at least one integration-style test per batch operation that uses real Project/Track/Clip objects and verifies the actual state change (e.g., clip.translation after move_all).

title: test_extras.py tests media_markers with fully mocked project/track/media
description: test_extras.py creates MagicMock objects for project, timeline, tracks, and media, then tests that media_markers() yields the right tuples. Since media_markers() is a simple generator that iterates project.timeline.tracks → track.medias → media.markers, the test is essentially verifying Python iteration works. A real project with markers would be a more meaningful test.
recommendation: Replace with a test using the `project` fixture: add a marker to a real clip and verify media_markers() yields it.

title: FrameStamp class has zero test coverage
description: The FrameStamp class in src/camtasia/frame_stamp.py has 7 public methods/properties (frame_time, time, __str__, __lt__, __add__, __sub__, _add) and the module-level _lcm() helper. No test file imports or references FrameStamp. This is a public API class with arithmetic operations that could have edge cases (different frame rates, negative frame numbers, zero frame rate).
recommendation: Add test_frame_stamp.py covering: basic frame_time calculation, time property, string representation, comparison, addition/subtraction with same and different frame rates, and _lcm edge cases.

title: CLI module (camtasia.cli) has no unit tests
description: The CLI module at src/camtasia/cli.py (7KB, ~200 lines) has no dedicated test file. While integration tests may exercise some CLI paths indirectly, there are no unit tests for argument parsing, error handling, or individual CLI commands. Design decision D-007 notes lazy imports, which is itself a testable behavior.
recommendation: Add test_cli.py with tests for: each CLI subcommand's argument parsing, error messages for invalid inputs, and the lazy-import behavior (importing camtasia without [cli] extras should not fail).

title: 14 test files use exact repr() string matching that breaks on formatting changes
description: Tests across 14 files assert exact repr() output strings, e.g., `assert repr(m) == "Marker(name='intro', time_seconds=3.00)"`. These break if: field order changes, precision formatting changes, or new fields are added to repr. Files include: test_protocols.py (5 assertions), test_str_methods.py (6), test_marker_consolidation.py, test_effects.py, test_track_operations.py, test_zoom_pan.py, test_visual_effects.py, test_captions.py, test_media_convenience.py, test_audiate_project.py, test_timeline_operations.py, test_project_summary.py, test_media_bin.py, test_project_statistics.py.
recommendation: For repr tests, prefer `assert 'intro' in repr(m)` or `assert repr(m).startswith('Marker(')` unless the exact format is part of the public API contract. Keep exact matching only for formats documented as stable.

title: test_annotation_favorites.py monkeypatches module-level _DEFAULT_FAVORITES_DIR instead of using tmp_path
description: TestCalloutAddToFavorites.test_saves_definition_via_clip monkeypatches camtasia.annotations.callouts._DEFAULT_FAVORITES_DIR to a tempfile.TemporaryDirectory, then restores it in a finally block. This is fragile: if the test fails between the monkeypatch and the finally, the module-level state is corrupted for subsequent tests. The test also mixes tempfile.TemporaryDirectory with the existing tmp_path fixture.
recommendation: Use pytest's monkeypatch fixture instead: `monkeypatch.setattr('camtasia.annotations.callouts._DEFAULT_FAVORITES_DIR', tmp_path)`. This auto-restores on test teardown regardless of pass/fail.

title: integration_helpers.py uses time.sleep(3) and time.sleep(timeout) for Camtasia synchronization
description: The _launch_and_scan() helper uses time.sleep(3) after pkill and time.sleep(timeout) to wait for Camtasia to parse the project. These are fixed delays that may be too short on slow machines (causing flaky failures) or too long on fast machines (wasting CI time). Design decision D-010 acknowledges serialization concerns but doesn't address the sleep-based synchronization.
recommendation: For the pkill wait, poll for process termination instead of sleeping. For the Camtasia parse wait, consider polling the log file for a 'ready' indicator or using a shorter initial sleep with retry logic. This is inherently difficult with a GUI app, so INFO-level if the current approach is considered acceptable.

title: test_import_trec_mocked.py uses importlib.reload() to swap pymediainfo mock, corrupting module state
description: TestTrecProbeRounding and TestTrecProbeHandlesDualRateSampleRate use importlib.reload(tp) to force the trec_probe module to re-import pymediainfo from the patched sys.modules. This is fragile: if a test fails before the finally block's reload(), the module remains in a corrupted state. Other tests importing trec_probe will get the mocked version.
recommendation: Use pytest's monkeypatch or unittest.mock.patch at the function level within trec_probe rather than reloading the entire module. For example, patch 'camtasia.media_bin.trec_probe.pymediainfo' directly.

title: test_downstream_typing.py runs mypy as a subprocess — should be a CI-only or integration test
description: test_downstream_typing.py spawns a subprocess to run mypy on a generated consumer script. This test: (1) requires mypy to be installed, (2) is slow (mypy startup), (3) depends on the Python version (skipif < 3.11), and (4) tests the type stubs, not runtime behavior. It's marked with skipif but not with any integration marker, so it runs in the regular unit test suite.
recommendation: Add a @pytest.mark.slow or @pytest.mark.typing marker so this test can be excluded from fast unit test runs. Consider moving it to a separate CI step.

title: Color parsing/conversion functions lack property-based tests
description: src/camtasia/color.py contains color parsing and conversion functions (hex to RGBA, RGBA to hex, etc.) that are pure functions with well-defined domains — ideal candidates for hypothesis property-based testing. The existing test_color.py uses only example-based tests. Properties like roundtrip (hex→rgba→hex), clamping bounds, and alpha channel handling would catch edge cases.
recommendation: Add hypothesis strategies for color values (0.0-1.0 floats, hex strings) and test roundtrip properties. The project already uses hypothesis in test_invariants.py and test_invariants_property.py.

title: Timing conversion functions (seconds_to_ticks, ticks_to_seconds) lack property-based tests
description: src/camtasia/timing.py contains seconds_to_ticks() and ticks_to_seconds() which are pure conversion functions. The existing tests use example-based assertions. A hypothesis test could verify the roundtrip property: ticks_to_seconds(seconds_to_ticks(x)) ≈ x for all valid inputs, catching floating-point edge cases.
recommendation: Add a hypothesis test: `@given(st.floats(min_value=0, max_value=1e6, allow_nan=False, allow_infinity=False))` testing the roundtrip property.

title: test_version.py only checks isinstance and tuple length, not actual version format
description: test_version.py verifies __version__ is a string containing '.' and __version_info__ is a 3-tuple. It doesn't verify the version follows semver (e.g., all parts are numeric), that __version__ and __version_info__ are consistent, or that the version is non-empty.
recommendation: Add assertions: `assert all(part.isdigit() for part in __version_info__)` and `assert __version__ == '.'.join(__version_info__)`.

title: test_marker_consolidation.py duplicates test_protocols.py Marker repr test
description: test_marker_consolidation.py::test_marker_repr_shows_seconds asserts `repr(m) == "Marker(name='intro', time_seconds=3.00)"`. test_protocols.py::test_marker_repr also asserts the same repr format for Marker. Both tests create a Marker with the same parameters and check the same output.
recommendation: Remove the duplicate repr test from test_marker_consolidation.py. The consolidation test should focus on verifying that the import path works (MarkerCompat is Marker), which it already does.

title: test_exports.py only checks hasattr and __all__ membership, not actual functionality
description: test_exports.py verifies that 5 clip type names are importable from camtasia and present in __all__. It doesn't test that the imported objects are the correct classes or that they can be instantiated. This is a smoke test that would pass even if the imports pointed to wrong classes.
recommendation: Add `assert issubclass(getattr(camtasia, name), BaseClip)` or similar type checks to verify the exports are the correct classes.

title: Majority of test functions lack docstrings explaining what behavior they verify
description: Across the test suite, most test functions have no docstring. While test names are generally descriptive (e.g., test_adds_media_to_specified_track), complex tests with multi-step setup would benefit from docstrings explaining the scenario and expected behavior. Notable exceptions: test_invariants.py and integration tests have good docstrings.
recommendation: Prioritize adding docstrings to: (1) tests with complex setup, (2) tests that verify non-obvious behavior, (3) regression tests (explain what bug they prevent). Don't add trivial docstrings to self-explanatory one-liner tests.

title: Many assert statements lack custom failure messages for non-obvious checks
description: Tests like `assert len(all_ids) == len(set(all_ids))` (test_invariants.py:64) do include a message, but many similar structural assertions across the suite don't. For example, `assert len(issues) == 1` in test_validate_all.py gives 'assert 0 == 1' on failure with no indication of what issues were found.
recommendation: For assertions on collections, include the collection in the message: `assert len(issues) == 1, f'Expected 1 issue, got {len(issues)}: {issues}'`. Focus on tests where the failure message would be ambiguous.

title: test_techsmith_roundtrip.py external /tmp dependency is consistent with D-010 serial integration pattern
description: Per design-decisions.md D-010, integration tests have specific environmental requirements. The /tmp sample scanning in test_techsmith_roundtrip.py follows a similar pattern where tests gracefully degrade when external resources are unavailable. Filing as INFO per D-010 guidance: the pattern is intentional but could be better documented.
recommendation: Add a comment in the test explaining the /tmp scanning rationale and how to populate the directories for extended testing.
